# Village Woodworking

> Custom, story-driven furniture and woodworking in Omaha and Lincoln, Nebraska. Heirloom-grade pieces designed with CAD precision, built by hand, and rooted in narrative.

- Website: https://villagewoodwork.com
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/villagewoodworking/
- Email: village.woodwrking@gmail.com
- Service Area: Omaha, NE · Lincoln, NE · surrounding metro areas
- Current Lead Time: 11–12 weeks
- Start a Project: https://villagewoodwork.com/intake.html
- Affiliation: Member of PRO Remodeling Omaha

## What Village Woodworking Does

Village Woodworking is a custom woodworking studio creating bespoke, one-of-a-kind furniture and built-ins for clients in the Omaha–Lincoln corridor. Every project is designed from scratch — no catalogs, no templates. The studio specializes in pieces that carry personal meaning: family heirlooms, statement furniture, office builds, and architectural woodwork.

The work combines traditional hand craftsmanship with CAD-driven design and CNC fabrication. Materials are intentionally sourced — premium domestic hardwoods, granite, and other materials selected for structural and narrative significance.

## Services

### Custom Furniture
Tables (dining, coffee, end tables), desks, shelving, seating, and sculptural builds. Each piece is designed in 3D CAD, prototyped, and built to the client's specific dimensions, materials, and story.

### Office Design & Builds
Custom cabinetry, desks, wall signs, and plaques for commercial and home office spaces. Designed to replace generic furniture with intentional, space-specific solutions.

### Wall Art & Signage
Dimensional wood panels, carved work, and branded signage for businesses and residences.

### Gifts & Keepsakes
Heirloom-quality objects — cutting boards, trays, keepsake boxes, and meaningful small-scale pieces designed for permanence.

### Cabinetry & Millwork
Prefabricated or fully custom cabinetry for kitchens, bathrooms, and built-in storage. Also includes custom millwork and architectural details.

### CAD Modeling & 3D Rendering
Standalone design services including 3D CAD models, rendered room visualizations, and 3D-printed prototypes for client review before fabrication.

## The Process

Village Woodworking follows a structured, six-step process from first conversation to final delivery:

1. **Concept & Moodboard** — Initial conversation and site visit. A moodboard is created to establish the design direction, material palette, and investment range. This step is collaborative and adapts to the client's level of involvement.
2. **3D CAD Modeling** — The design is modeled in vector-based CAD software. Proportions, joinery, and structural decisions are made here. Clients receive a 3D-printed prototype for furniture projects, along with a formal quote.
3. **Contract** — A standard contract is provided. A 10% deposit books the project; an initial payment of approximately 50% is due when work begins.
4. **Material Sourcing** — Premium materials (hardwoods, granite, hardware) are acquired based on the approved design. Material selection is intentional and often tied to the project's narrative.
5. **Creation** — The piece is built with full attention — one active project at a time. Combines hand tool work with CNC machining and digital fabrication.
6. **Delivery & Install** — Final delivery, installation, and walkthrough. Every project includes a flexible warranty.

## Project Types and Budget Ranges

Village Woodworking works across a range of budgets and project scales:

- Under $100 — Small gifts, cutting boards, trays
- $100–$500 — Keepsakes, small furniture, wall art
- $500–$2,000 — Custom tables, desks, shelving units
- $2,000–$5,000 — Complex furniture, office builds, cabinetry
- $5,000+ — Large-scale commissions, multi-piece projects, architectural millwork

## Location and Service Area

Village Woodworking is based in the Omaha–Lincoln metropolitan area of Nebraska. The studio serves residential and commercial clients across Omaha, Lincoln, Elkhorn, Papillion, La Vista, Bellevue, Gretna, Bennington, and surrounding communities in eastern Nebraska and western Iowa.

## Design Philosophy

Village Woodworking creates objects that are structurally confident, materially honest, and narratively grounded. The studio draws from influences including mid-century modern design, brutalism, wabi-sabi, and the sculptural furniture traditions of Isamu Noguchi and George Nakashima. Technology (CAD, CNC, 3D printing) is used in service of craft — never as spectacle.

The guiding principle: every piece should carry a story, serve a purpose, and outlast its maker.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Do you work from a catalog?**
No. Every project is designed from scratch based on the client's story, space, and goals.

**What is the current lead time?**
11–12 weeks from contract signing to delivery.

**How does payment work?**
10% deposit to book. ~50% when work begins. Balance on completion. Detailed invoicing and cost documentation available on request.

**Can I make changes during the build?**
Yes. Change orders are welcomed and built into the process.

**What does the warranty cover?**
Every project includes an extensive and flexible warranty.

**What areas do you serve?**
Omaha, Lincoln, and the surrounding Nebraska metro area.
